jobs.title: AIR POLLUTION SPECIALIST
jobs.description:
Job Posting: Air Pollution Specialist



State Air Resources Board


JC\-525557* 

Air Pollution Specialist
  

AIR POLLUTION SPECIALIST
$5,918\.00 \- $11,455\.00 per Month
  

New to State candidates will be hired into the minimum salary of the classification or minimum of alternate range when applicable.


**Final Filing Date:** **8/4/2026**


### **Job Description and Duties**


The Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Emissions Data Quality Assurance Section in the Program Planning and Management Branch has a vacant Air Pollution Specialist (APS) position for which it is seeking highly competent candidates with experience and expertise in natural and working lands GHG quantification and emissions reduction methods. The Section is responsible for ensuring the impartiality and quality of third\-party verifications and will oversee third\-party verifications performed as required by Senate Bill 27: Carbon sequestration: state goals: natural and working lands: registry of projects (SB 27\).  

Under direction of the Air Resources I, the APS applies scientific methods and principles in the identification, study, and solution of air pollution problems. The APS designs, conducts, and evaluates methods to reduce GHG emissions from natural and working lands. The APS specifically focuses on forests, floodplains and wetlands, coastal habitats, agriculture, and urban lands. This candidate will work in coordination with the California Natural Resources Agency to develop the Natural and Working Lands Climate Smart Strategy, as required by SB 27, and will also play a lead role in establishing carbon dioxide removal targets for 2030 and beyond, while considering the Natural and Working Lands Climate Smart Strategy, science\-based data, cost\-effectiveness, and technological feasibility in setting the targets.  

The candidate will support CARB’s efforts in developing policy through technical and policy analysis, and quantifying the costs, benefits, and equity implications of natural and working lands policies. Additionally, the candidate will identify research needs that advance CARB’s understanding of cutting\-edge issues in natural and working lands. The candidate will engage and collaborate with relevant stakeholders including, but not limited to, public agencies, research institutions, equity stakeholders, developers, industry, and other CARB staff, management, and executive leadership teams. This candidate must understand the complexities of natural and working lands and the roles that policy, technology, and the natural environment play. The candidate should also be aware of the social equity implications of natural and workin glands policies and be able to develop strategies accordingly.
You will find additional information about the job in the **Duty Statement**.


### **Working Conditions**


This position may be eligible for hybrid in\-office work and in\-state telework. The amount of telework is at the agency's discretion and is based on the California Air Resources Board’s (CARB) current telework policy. While the CARB may support telework, some in\-person attendance is also required.


The positions at the CARB may be eligible for telework with in\-person attendance based on the operational needs of the position under Government Code 14200 for eligible applicants residing in California, subject to the candidate meeting telework eligibility criteria outlined in the CalEPA telework policy and/or future program need. Employees not residing in California are not eligible for telework. Regardless of hybrid telework eligibility, all employees may be required to report to the position’s designated headquarters location at their own expense, as indicated on their duty statement.


Effective July 1, 2025, the California Department of Human Resources (CalHR) implemented the Personal Leave Program 2025 (PLP 2025\). PLP 2025 directs that each employee shall receive a 3 percent reduction in pay in exchange for 5 hours PLP 2025 leave credits, monthly. The salary range(s) included in the job advertisement do not reflect the 3 percent reduction in pay.


* Position located in a high\-rise building.
* Requires being stationary, consistent with office work, for extended periods.
* Standard office environment (artificial lighting, controlled temperature, etc.).
* Daily use of a personal computer, office equipment, and/or telephone.
* Travel may be required.

  
### **Position Details**


**Job Code \#:**
JC\-525557
**Position \#(s):**
673\-450\-3887\-083
**Working Title:**
**Air Pollution Specialist**
**Classification:**
AIR POLLUTION SPECIALIST
$5,918\.00 \- $7,059\.00 A
$7,089\.00 \- $8,872\.00 B
$9,158\.00 \- $11,455\.00 C
New to State candidates will be hired into the minimum salary of the classification or minimum of alternate range when applicable.
**\# of Positions:**
Multiple
**Work Location:**
Sacramento County
**Telework:**
Hybrid
**Job Type:**
Permanent, Full Time
### **Department Information**


This employer participates in E\-Verify and will provide the federal government with your Form I\-9 information to confirm that you are authorized to work in the U.S.; for more information about E\-Verify, please go to www.dhs.gov/e\-verify


CARB values diversity at all levels of the organization and is committed to fostering an environment where employees from various backgrounds, cultures, and personal experiences are welcomed and can thrive. CARB believes the diversity of our employees collectively makes up one of the world’s most respected environmental organizations. CARB offers various modern interview options, including remote phone and video interviews. Join CARB and help us improve the lives of all Californians.


Department Website: http://www.arb.ca.gov
### **Special Requirements**


* The position(s) require(s) a Background Investigation be cleared prior to being hired.
### **Application Instructions**


Completed applications and all required documents must be received or postmarked by the Final Filing D

jobs.title: SENIOR ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENTIST (SUPERVISORY)
jobs.description:
Job Posting: SCE Supervisor



Department of Fish and Wildlife


JC\-525540* 

SCE Supervisor
  

SENIOR ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENTIST (SUPERVISORY)
$10,854\.00 \- $13,493\.00 per Month
  

New to State candidates will be hired into the minimum salary of the classification or minimum of alternate range when applicable.


**Final Filing Date:** **8/4/2026**


### **Job Description and Duties**


Are you ready to take on a challenge and lead a dynamic team working on high\-profile environmental projects? The California Department of Fish and Wildlife (CDFW) is seeking a Limited Term Senior Environmental Scientist (Supervisory) to oversee the Southern California Edison (SCE) workload in our Habitat Conservation Planning Program. If you are a self\-motivated individual who thrives in fast\-paced, complex work environments and is passionate about conservation and environmental protection, we want to hear from you!  

  

  

About the Role: This is a working supervisor position where you will supervise and conduct environmental review, permitting, and conservation planning functions for SCE projects. You will play a key role in ensuring compliance with the California Endangered Species Act (CESA), California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA), and Lake and Streambed Alteration (LSA) agreements, working to maintain a balance between environmental protection and project progress.  

  

  

CDFW may be serving as the Lead or Responsible Agency under CEQA in order to issue permits, in this role you will initiate, execute, and manage contracts with SCE, coordinate with consultants, and oversee the preparation of critical documents. You will guide and support the issuance of permits, ensure timely completion of environmental reviews, facilitate mitigation efforts, and assess the success of conservation measures across multiple regions.  

  

  

What You Will Do:
\- Lead a team of Senior Environmental Scientist (Specialists) in environmental review, permitting, and conservation planning for SCE projects.
\- Oversee the development and certification of CEQA documents for SCE's permits, including a Master Lake and Streambed Alteration Agreement (LSAA) and possibly CESA incidental take permits for projects across Regions 4, 5, and 6\.
\- Coordinate with regional and statewide staff to ensure project goals are met on time and in compliance with environmental laws and regulations.
\- Provide leadership and mentorship to your team, fostering a collaborative environment and driving success in complex, multi\-regional projects.
  

What We Are Looking For: We need someone who can take on a challenge, manage multiple complex projects, and ensure deadlines are met while maintaining the highest standards of environmental stewardship. If you are a problem\-solver with strong leadership skills and a passion for conservation, this could be the perfect role for you!  

  

  

In order to be eligible for this position, you must either be transfer eligible or take and pass the following exam.  

Class Title: Senior Environmental Scientist (Supervisory)/Exam Bulletin  

  

  

**This position is posted in the following counties:** *Orange/Santa Barbara/San Diego counties*. **Please apply to each county in which you are interested in working.**  

  

**This position is a 12\-month Limited Term (LT) and may be extended up to 24 months or become permanent.**  

During this 12\-month Limited Term assignment, you will receive full health, dental, and vision benefits! You will also earn State Service and accrue monthly leave credits.  

  

  

  

  

You will find additional information about the job in the **Duty Statement**.


### **Working Conditions**


The office will be located in the Seal Beach, Santa Barbara or San Diego office and must be able to travel up to 20% to attend site visits throughout the South Coast Region, including Santa Barbara, Ventura, Los Angeles, and Orange Counties. Travel outside the region up to 5% will also be required. The position involves using a computer keyboard and Microsoft Office software for up to eight hours per day, as well as performing office tasks that require sitting, standing, and walking between locations. The role includes coordinating and participating in meetings and conference calls, attending in\-person workgroups with stakeholders, and engaging with local, state, and federal agencies in the South Coast Region, as well as Regions 4 and 6\. Field site visits will be necessary, and the incumbent must be able to walk on uneven, hilly terrain. As a condition of employment, the incumbent may be required to wear a uniform in accordance with CDFW’s Operations Manual (Section 12901\) during field surveys and public interactions, both within and outside the Region as directed by the supervisor. A valid driver's license is required travel and field site visits to remote or rural areas where alternative transportation is not feasible. Telework is up to the discretion and approval of the supervisor and will be consistent with current telework policies.


  
### **Position Details**


**Job Code \#:**
JC\-525540
**Position \#(s):**
565\-531\-0764\-905
**Working Title:**
**SCE Supervisor**
**Classification:**
SENIOR ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENTIST (SUPERVISORY)
$10,854\.00 \- $13,493\.00
New to State candidates will be hired into the minimum salary of the classification or minimum of alternate range when applicable.
**\# of Positions:**
1
**Work Location:**
Orange County
**Telework:**
Hybrid
**Job Type:**
12 Month Limited Term \- Full Time
### **Department Information**


Department of Fish and Wildlife (CDFW) employees are committed to managing and protecting California’s diverse wildlife and the habitats upon which they depend.  

California is one of the most biodiverse places on the planet. As such, CDFW values diverse employees working together to protect nature for all Californians. CDFW is committed to fostering an inclusive work environment where all backgrounds, cultures and persona

jobs.title: Environmental Health Specialist II
jobs.description:
**Position Information**
------------------------


**We are seeking an Environmental Health Specialist II to join the Department of Health Services and help advance the health and safety of Sonoma County!**

**Starting salary up to $60\.53/hour ($126,333/year) and a competitive total compensation package!\***


The Department of Health Services is currently recruiting to fill one full\-time Environmental Health Specialist (EHS) position at either the Trainee, I, or II level. If you are interested in being considered at the different levels of this opportunity, you must apply to the separate concurrent recruitments being conducted for each level.

**Please Note:** As mandated by the State of California's Health and Safety Code, the Environmental Health Specialist II level requires possession of a valid certificate of registration as an Environmental Health Specialist. For more information on the requirements and process to obtain a Registered Environmental Health Specialist certificate in California, please visit the CA Registered Environmental Health Specialist Program website.  

  

**About the Position**
We're looking for a motivated individual to join our team as an Environmental Health Specialist II who wants to advance their career in Environmental Health. This role is vital to protecting public health, serving the community, and supporting the functions of the Environmental Health Section. As an Environmental Health Specialist II, you will:

* Perform inspections and investigations across a wide range of programs, including but not limited to: Food Facilities, Recreational Facilities, Cannabis Facilities, Medical Waste Facilities, Water Systems, Beach monitoring, Solid Waste, Body Art, Childhood Lead Prevention, Storm Water, Organized Camps, Detention Facilities, and Disaster Preparedness
* Ensure compliance with County and State health and safety regulations through routine inspections, complaint responses, documentation, enforcement actions, and public education
* Prepare thorough inspection reports, interpret relevant health codes, and communicate findings and corrective actions to stakeholders
* Interact regularly with the public, businesses, and regulatory partners, often providing guidance and support in diverse environments
* Be a subject matter expert for the work of the program they are assigned to

  

As the ideal candidate for this position, you will be dedicated to helping promote a healthy and safe environment, be an excellent communicator, and possess many of the following:* Experience with sanitation science, public health principles, and relevant environmental health laws and regulations
* The ability to adapt to an evolving public health landscape and perform a broad range of functions to meet Departmental operational needs
* Experience identifying potential issues, researching these issues, preparing supporting documentation, and presenting these observations accurately and effectively
* Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Outlook, Excel, PowerPoint) and general computer skills
* Effective written and verbal communication skills, including report writing, documentation, and presentations
* The ability to think critically, research regulatory issues, and analyze field findings
* A strong sense of empathy for the clients you will work with
* The ability to build collaborative relationships with a diverse community, team members, and partner agencies

  

Work is primarily performed Monday through Friday, with occasional evenings and weekends to inspect temporary events or respond to complaints. Many inspections are outdoors and require working in varying environmental conditions, including beaches, waste sites, and food service facilities.

**What We Offer**


Working at the County of Sonoma offers expansive opportunities for growth and development, the ability to be a part of a challenging and rewarding work environment, and the satisfaction of knowing you're working to better our communities. You can also look forward to flexible work arrangements and excellent benefits\* including:

* **Salary Advancement** \- A salary increase after 1,040 hours (6 months when working full\-time) for good work performance; eligibility for a salary increase for good performance every year thereafter, until reaching the top of the salary range
* **Paid Time Off** \- Competitive vacation and sick leave accruals and 12 paid holidays
* **County Paid Health Premium Contributions** \- 100% premium contribution for the majority of employee\-only and employee \+ family health plan options
* **Staff Development/Wellness** \- Annual benefit allowances of up to $850 and ongoing education/training opportunities
* **Post\-Retirement Health Reimbursement Arrangement** \- County contributions to help fund post\-retirement health insurance/benefits
* **Retirement** \- A pension fully integrated with Social Security
* **Paid Parental Leave** \- May be eligible for up to 8 weeks (320 hours) after 12 months of County employment
* **Additional Benefits** \- Excellent dental, vision, disability, life insurance, employee assistance program, professional development, and more
* **Student Loan Debt Relief** \- County employees may be eligible for Public Service Loan Forgiveness through the U.S. Department of Education
* Salary is negotiable within the established range. Benefits described herein do not represent a contract and may be changed without notice. Additional information can be found in the Engineers and Scientists of California Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) and our Employee Benefits Directory.


This recruitment is being conducted to fill an Environmental Health Specialist II position in the Department of Health Services. This employment list may also be used to fill future full\-time, part\-time, or extra\-help (temporary) positions as they occur during the active status of the list.

**Application submissions require the Supplemental Questionnaire to be completed.**


**Minimum Qual

* **Retirement** \- A pension fully integrated with Social Security
* **Paid Parental Leave** \- May be eligible for up to 8 weeks (320 hours) after 12 months of County employment
* **Additional Benefits** \- Excellent dental, vision, disability, life insurance, employee assistance program, professional development, and more
* **Student Loan Debt Relief** \- County employees may be eligible for Public Service Loan Forgiveness through the U.S. Department of Education

jobs.title: Environmental Scientist
jobs.description:
**Job Overview:**



APTIM Environmental \& Infrastructure, LLC is seeking a mid\-level Environmental Scientist on a full\-time basis to work remotely from Oregon to participate in a variety of environmental compliance tasks in support of environmental compliance and permitting review programs. The successful candidate will prepare technical and compliance reports and review reports completed by other personnel, and lead training of other staff members. Assist in preparation of proposals, budgets, vendor/subcontractor selection, and other contract related items. Conduct technical and regulatory analyses and review analyses of junior personnel. Perform diverse fieldwork, possibly including sampling inspections and audits. Apply expertise to both standard and unique situations, ensuring environmental standards are met across projects. Be expected to maintain current knowledge of environmental regulations and emerging developments. This role requires both independent work and collaboration with multidisciplinary teams to ensure compliance and provide clients with practical, science\-based solutions. Responsibilities may also include supervising junior staff and participating in leadership development opportunities. This position will primarily be remote but may also require travel to client facilities, conduct environmental compliance reviews/audits and/or gather related data.


**Key Responsibilities/Accountabilities:**


* Executing and managing various work scopes, while meeting project schedules and budgets.
* Collection and interpretation/evaluation of scientific data.
* Executes field work within project schedule and budgetary goals and supervises subcontractors.
* Use various environmental databases and software to research project/site requirements and document the results in a technical report.
* Work independently and as part of a project team and be able to adjust to changing conditions or client requirements. Supervises junior staff on more complex work tasks.
* Adheres to established health and safety policies and procedures and executes work in a safe and conscientious manner.
* Understands technical concepts, evaluation results, plans, reports, and ensures conformance to applicable technical standards.
* Interfacing with client personnel at a corporate and facility level.
* Has in\-depth experience, knowledge, and skills in own discipline.
* Flexible and willing to support other projects as needed.
* Drafts technical documents for regulatory agencies and private clients.
* Providing training, guidance and oversight to junior staff.
* Applies knowledge and skills to more complex situations.
* Build and maintain successful client relationships.
* Work with clients to evaluate proposed projects for anticipated impacts, define type of environmental requirements needed and prepare proposals.
* Communications with regulatory agencies.
* Time management to ensure a quality product is prepared to meet project deadlines.
* Applies knowledge and skills to a wide range of standard and nonstandard situations.
* Following defined processes explicitly while also being able to identify and accommodate new or changing scenarios as well as developing processes for new activities.
* Prior experience or training regarding environmental compliance and related federal and state environmental regulations required.
* Experience with auditing is preferred.


**Basic Qualifications:**


* Bachelor's degree in environmental science, a core science (such as geology, chemistry, physics, or biology), engineering, or related area required.
* Related work experience of 4\-6 years.
* Must have strong written and oral communication skills.
* Must have strong organizational skills.
* Strong computer skills required. Must have demonstrated strength and proficiency with Microsoft applications such as MS Excel, MS Word, SharePoint Online, and ability to learn other software applications.
* May be asked to work in the field and travel to complete work.


**About APTIM**



APTIM enhances the quality of the environment, wellbeing of people, resilience of communities, and opportunity for employees to make a difference, not just a living. A leading professional solutions firm, we specialize in environmental, sustainability, resilience, and infrastructure solutions. Our team of experts provide these integrated solutions for forward\-thinking government agencies, commercial and industrial clients, and energy customers. Together, we create sustainable and resilient communities for all.



What you can expect from APTIM:


* Work that is worthy of your time and talent
* Respect and flexibility to live a full life at work and at home
* Dogged determination to deliver for our clients and communities
* A voice in making our company better
* Investment into your personal and professional development


As of the date of this posting, a good faith estimate of the current pay range for this position is $80,000 to $92,000 per year. Compensation depends on several factors including: experience, education, key skills, geographic location of the position, client requirements, external market competitiveness, and internal equity among other employees within APTIM.


**Employee Benefits**


APTIM Environmental \& Infrastructure, llp committed to providing an extensive range of benefits that protect and promote the health and financial well\-being of our employees and their families through the APTIM Benefits Marketplace https://aptim.makeityoursource.com/.


* Medical, vision, and dental insurance: Through the marketplace, our employees can choose benefits from five metallic levels and several carriers to find the right benefits that work for them in their location.
* Company paid and optional Life insurance
* Short\-term and long\-term disability insurance
* Accident, Critical Illness, and Hospital Indemnity coverage
* Paid holidays, vacation, and sick leave (eligibility based on company policy and applicable law)
* 401(k) APTIM of

jobs.title: Senior Environmental Scientist
jobs.description:
**About EHS Support**

  

EHS Support is a specialty environmental consulting firm that provides solutions to complex environmental, health, and safety challenges across a range of market sectors and industries. With experts located throughout the United States, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, the United Kingdom, and South Africa, we form long\-lasting partnerships with our clients through a culture of excellence, innovation, work ethic, and integrity. We focus on hiring and retaining talented, motivated people who enjoy collaborating with and delivering value to our clients. Our team does more than identify problems— we provide solutions.

 **Location:** Brisbane, QLD \- flexible home, office and field\-based role

**About the Role**


We are seeking an experienced environmental scientist or engineer to join our Queensland team and play a leading role in the delivery of environmental assessment, compliance, remediation and environmental management projects.


This role is suited to an experienced consultant who enjoys technical problem solving, project leadership and working with clients to develop practical and defensible environmental solutions. The position offers exposure to a broad range of projects and sectors, including energy, resources, infrastructure, manufacturing, chemicals and heavy industry.


While a strong background in contaminated land assessment and management is important, we are particularly interested in adaptable practitioners who can apply their technical skills across a range of environmental disciplines, including compliance, approvals, environmental due diligence, environmental management and remediation.


The preferred location for this role is Brisbane; however, other Queensland locations will be considered for suitably experienced candidates. The role involves a flexible mix of office, home and field\-based work, together with periodic travel to project sites throughout Queensland and Australia.

**Typical responsibilities:**

* Lead technical delivery of environmental assessment, compliance, remediation and environmental management projects.
* Plan, coordinate and manage environmental investigations, including soil, groundwater, surface water and vapour assessment programs.
* Review, interpret and evaluate environmental data and develop technically robust and commercially practical recommendations.
* Author and review technical reports and project deliverables, including:


 o Preliminary and detailed site investigations


 o Contaminated land assessments


 o Remediation action plans and validation reports


 o Environmental management plans


 o Compliance and monitoring report


 o Environmental approvals and supporting documentation

* Provide technical leadership and quality assurance for work completed by project team members.
* Lead the technical delivery and project execution of environmental assessment, compliance, remediation and environmental management projects.
* Coordinate and supervise subcontractors and specialist service providers during field investigations and remediation works.
* Engage directly with clients, regulators, contractors and other stakeholders to support project delivery and resolve technical issues.
* Contribute to proposal preparation, scope development and cost estimation for new opportunities.
* Support the development of junior and intermediate staff through technical guidance, coaching and project leadership.
* Contribute to technical innovation and continuous improvement initiatives within the business.


As a growing business, this role provides opportunities to expand technical leadership responsibilities, strengthen client relationships, contribute to service development and play a meaningful role in the continued growth of the Queensland practice.

**Position Requirements:**


This opportunity is suited to a motivated and adaptable environmental professional who combines strong technical capability with project leadership and client management skills. The successful candidate will demonstrate:

* Strong technical knowledge of contaminated land assessment, remediation and environmental management principles.
* Excellent analytical and critical\-thinking skills, with the ability to develop practical and defensible solutions to complex environmental challenges.
* Exceptional written communication skills and demonstrated experience preparing high\-quality technical reports and client deliverables.
* Strong verbal communication and stakeholder engagement skills.
* Proven ability to manage projects, coordinate multidisciplinary teams and deliver successful outcomes.
* Ability to work independently while providing leadership and direction within collaborative project teams.
* Sound professional judgement and a practical approach to balancing technical, regulatory and commercial considerations.
* A strong commitment to health, safety and environmental performance.

**Qualifications for this role include:**

* Bachelor's or Master's degree in Soil Science, Geology, Hydrogeology, Environmental Science, Environmental Engineering or a related discipline.
* Approximately 7\-10 years' experience in environmental consulting or a closely related technical role.
* Demonstrated experience undertaking and managing contaminated land investigations, environmental site assessments and remediation projects.
* Strong understanding of the ASC NEPM and Queensland contaminated land regulatory framework.
* Experience managing environmental field programs, including soil, groundwater and surface water investigations.
* Demonstrated experience leading the preparation and review of high\-quality technical reports and client deliverables.
* Proven project management experience, including management of scope, schedule, budget and project risks.
* Experience working across multiple environmental service areas such as compliance, approvals, environmental management, due diligence or remediation would be highly regarded.
* Current Australian drive

new_jobs.title: AIR POLLUTION SPECIALIST
new_jobs.description:
Job Posting: Air Pollution Specialist



State Air Resources Board


JC\-525557* 

Air Pollution Specialist
  

AIR POLLUTION SPECIALIST
$5,918\.00 \- $11,455\.00 per Month
  

New to State candidates will be hired into the minimum salary of the classification or minimum of alternate range when applicable.


**Final Filing Date:** **8/4/2026**


### **Job Description and Duties**


The Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Emissions Data Quality Assurance Section in the Program Planning and Management Branch has a vacant Air Pollution Specialist (APS) position for which it is seeking highly competent candidates with experience and expertise in natural and working lands GHG quantification and emissions reduction methods. The Section is responsible for ensuring the impartiality and quality of third\-party verifications and will oversee third\-party verifications performed as required by Senate Bill 27: Carbon sequestration: state goals: natural and working lands: registry of projects (SB 27\).  

Under direction of the Air Resources I, the APS applies scientific methods and principles in the identification, study, and solution of air pollution problems. The APS designs, conducts, and evaluates methods to reduce GHG emissions from natural and working lands. The APS specifically focuses on forests, floodplains and wetlands, coastal habitats, agriculture, and urban lands. This candidate will work in coordination with the California Natural Resources Agency to develop the Natural and Working Lands Climate Smart Strategy, as required by SB 27, and will also play a lead role in establishing carbon dioxide removal targets for 2030 and beyond, while considering the Natural and Working Lands Climate Smart Strategy, science\-based data, cost\-effectiveness, and technological feasibility in setting the targets.  

The candidate will support CARB’s efforts in developing policy through technical and policy analysis, and quantifying the costs, benefits, and equity implications of natural and working lands policies. Additionally, the candidate will identify research needs that advance CARB’s understanding of cutting\-edge issues in natural and working lands. The candidate will engage and collaborate with relevant stakeholders including, but not limited to, public agencies, research institutions, equity stakeholders, developers, industry, and other CARB staff, management, and executive leadership teams. This candidate must understand the complexities of natural and working lands and the roles that policy, technology, and the natural environment play. The candidate should also be aware of the social equity implications of natural and workin glands policies and be able to develop strategies accordingly.
You will find additional information about the job in the **Duty Statement**.


### **Working Conditions**


This position may be eligible for hybrid in\-office work and in\-state telework. The amount of telework is at the agency's discretion and is based on the California Air Resources Board’s (CARB) current telework policy. While the CARB may support telework, some in\-person attendance is also required.


The positions at the CARB may be eligible for telework with in\-person attendance based on the operational needs of the position under Government Code 14200 for eligible applicants residing in California, subject to the candidate meeting telework eligibility criteria outlined in the CalEPA telework policy and/or future program need. Employees not residing in California are not eligible for telework. Regardless of hybrid telework eligibility, all employees may be required to report to the position’s designated headquarters location at their own expense, as indicated on their duty statement.


Effective July 1, 2025, the California Department of Human Resources (CalHR) implemented the Personal Leave Program 2025 (PLP 2025\). PLP 2025 directs that each employee shall receive a 3 percent reduction in pay in exchange for 5 hours PLP 2025 leave credits, monthly. The salary range(s) included in the job advertisement do not reflect the 3 percent reduction in pay.


* Position located in a high\-rise building.
* Requires being stationary, consistent with office work, for extended periods.
* Standard office environment (artificial lighting, controlled temperature, etc.).
* Daily use of a personal computer, office equipment, and/or telephone.
* Travel may be required.

new_jobs.title: SENIOR ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENTIST (SUPERVISORY)
new_jobs.description:
Job Posting: SCE Supervisor



Department of Fish and Wildlife


JC\-525540* 

SCE Supervisor
  

SENIOR ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENTIST (SUPERVISORY)
$10,854\.00 \- $13,493\.00 per Month
  

New to State candidates will be hired into the minimum salary of the classification or minimum of alternate range when applicable.


**Final Filing Date:** **8/4/2026**


### **Job Description and Duties**


Are you ready to take on a challenge and lead a dynamic team working on high\-profile environmental projects? The California Department of Fish and Wildlife (CDFW) is seeking a Limited Term Senior Environmental Scientist (Supervisory) to oversee the Southern California Edison (SCE) workload in our Habitat Conservation Planning Program. If you are a self\-motivated individual who thrives in fast\-paced, complex work environments and is passionate about conservation and environmental protection, we want to hear from you!  

  

  

About the Role: This is a working supervisor position where you will supervise and conduct environmental review, permitting, and conservation planning functions for SCE projects. You will play a key role in ensuring compliance with the California Endangered Species Act (CESA), California Environmental Quality Act (CEQA), and Lake and Streambed Alteration (LSA) agreements, working to maintain a balance between environmental protection and project progress.  

  

  

CDFW may be serving as the Lead or Responsible Agency under CEQA in order to issue permits, in this role you will initiate, execute, and manage contracts with SCE, coordinate with consultants, and oversee the preparation of critical documents. You will guide and support the issuance of permits, ensure timely completion of environmental reviews, facilitate mitigation efforts, and assess the success of conservation measures across multiple regions.  

  

  

What You Will Do:
\- Lead a team of Senior Environmental Scientist (Specialists) in environmental review, permitting, and conservation planning for SCE projects.
\- Oversee the development and certification of CEQA documents for SCE's permits, including a Master Lake and Streambed Alteration Agreement (LSAA) and possibly CESA incidental take permits for projects across Regions 4, 5, and 6\.
\- Coordinate with regional and statewide staff to ensure project goals are met on time and in compliance with environmental laws and regulations.
\- Provide leadership and mentorship to your team, fostering a collaborative environment and driving success in complex, multi\-regional projects.
  

What We Are Looking For: We need someone who can take on a challenge, manage multiple complex projects, and ensure deadlines are met while maintaining the highest standards of environmental stewardship. If you are a problem\-solver with strong leadership skills and a passion for conservation, this could be the perfect role for you!  

  

  

In order to be eligible for this position, you must either be transfer eligible or take and pass the following exam.  

Class Title: Senior Environmental Scientist (Supervisory)/Exam Bulletin  

  

  

**This position is posted in the following counties:** *Orange/Santa Barbara/San Diego counties*. **Please apply to each county in which you are interested in working.**  

  

**This position is a 12\-month Limited Term (LT) and may be extended up to 24 months or become permanent.**  

During this 12\-month Limited Term assignment, you will receive full health, dental, and vision benefits! You will also earn State Service and accrue monthly leave credits.  

  

  

  

  

You will find additional information about the job in the **Duty Statement**.


### **Working Conditions**


The office will be located in the Seal Beach, Santa Barbara or San Diego office and must be able to travel up to 20% to attend site visits throughout the South Coast Region, including Santa Barbara, Ventura, Los Angeles, and Orange Counties. Travel outside the region up to 5% will also be required. The position involves using a computer keyboard and Microsoft Office software for up to eight hours per day, as well as performing office tasks that require sitting, standing, and walking between locations. The role includes coordinating and participating in meetings and conference calls, attending in\-person workgroups with stakeholders, and engaging with local, state, and federal agencies in the South Coast Region, as well as Regions 4 and 6\. Field site visits will be necessary, and the incumbent must be able to walk on uneven, hilly terrain. As a condition of employment, the incumbent may be required to wear a uniform in accordance with CDFW’s Operations Manual (Section 12901\) during field surveys and public interactions, both within and outside the Region as directed by the supervisor. A valid driver's license is required travel and field site visits to remote or rural areas where alternative transportation is not feasible. Telework is up to the discretion and approval of the supervisor and will be consistent with current telework policies.


  
### **Position Details**


**Job Code \#:**
JC\-525540
**Position \#(s):**
565\-531\-0764\-905
**Working Title:**
**SCE Supervisor**
**Classification:**
SENIOR ENVIRONMENTAL SCIENTIST (SUPERVISORY)
$10,854\.00 \- $13,493\.00
New to State candidates will be hired into the minimum salary of the classification or minimum of alternate range when applicable.
**\# of Positions:**
1
**Work Location:**
Orange County
**Telework:**
Hybrid
**Job Type:**
12 Month Limited Term \- Full Time
### **Department Information**


Department of Fish and Wildlife (CDFW) employees are committed to managing and protecting California’s diverse wildlife and the habitats upon which they depend.  

California is one of the most biodiverse places on the planet. As such, CDFW values diverse employees working together to protect nature for all Californians. CDFW is committed to fostering an inclusive work environment where all backgrounds, cultures and persona
